_meta.schema: exact: lowercased-basename -> {base, family, type, summary, good_for, notes}. prefix: ORDERED list of {match (case-insensitive regex against the lowercased basename), base, family, type, summary, good_for, notes, categories?}. Exact wins over prefix; among prefix entries the FIRST match wins, so specific models precede the base-architecture families they belong to. `categories` (optional) restricts an entry to the listed folder_paths categories. `base` is the architecture for compatibility (which LoRA/VAE/ControlNet fits which checkpoint).
_meta.sources: Model families described at the family level (lineage, base architecture, typical use) — filenames are arbitrary so 1:1 enumeration is impossible. Compiled from public model-card / release information. Contributions welcome via PR; never fabricate — omit a field rather than guess.
_meta.disclaimer: Heuristic name matching. A filename is not authoritative about a model's true base; treat badges as a hint, not a guarantee.

ae.safetensors.summary: The Flux.1 / SD3-family variational autoencoder, distributed simply as ae.safetensors. Required to decode latents from Flux and SD3 diffusion models.

sdxl_vae.safetensors.summary: Stability's official SDXL autoencoder. Use the fp16-fixed build if you see black/NaN images on fp16.
sdxl_vae.safetensors.good_for: Any SDXL checkpoint that ships without a baked-in VAE

vae-ft-mse-840000-ema-pruned.safetensors.summary: The community-standard SD1.5 autoencoder, fine-tuned to 840k steps with an MSE objective. Sharper, less washed-out colour than the base SD1.5 VAE.
vae-ft-mse-840000-ema-pruned.safetensors.good_for: Almost every SD1.5 checkpoint without a baked-in VAE

prefix.summary: Black Forest Labs' 12B rectified-flow transformer, timestep-distilled for very few steps. Apache-2.0 licensed.
prefix.good_for: Fast Flux generation at 1-4 steps; commercial-friendly licence

prefix.summary: Black Forest Labs' 12B rectified-flow transformer, guidance-distilled. The quality flagship of the open Flux line (non-commercial licence).
prefix.good_for: High-quality Flux generation at 20-30 steps; needs a t5xxl + clip_l encoder and the ae VAE

prefix.summary: A Flux.1 variant (Krea = aesthetic tune; Kontext = in-context image editing).
prefix.good_for: Flux workflows targeting that specific variant

prefix.summary: RunDiffusion's popular photoreal SDXL merge line. Strong all-round realism with reliable prompt adherence.
prefix.notes: Newer Juggernaut releases also ship a Flux.1 variant — check the version.

prefix.summary: Lykon's versatile semi-realistic line, released across SD1.5, SDXL and Turbo/Lightning bases. Bridges photoreal and illustrative styles.
prefix.notes: Base depends on the build (SD1.5 vs SDXL vs Turbo) — read the filename's version.

prefix.summary: SG161222's photoreal SDXL fine-tune. Skin, lighting and portrait realism are its strengths.
prefix.good_for: Photorealistic portraits and scenes on SDXL

prefix.summary: SG161222's long-running SD1.5 photoreal line — one of the most-used realism checkpoints of the SD1.5 era.
A popular SD1.5 photoreal merge known for natural skin and lighting.
prefix.notes: An SDXL/XL build also exists — check the version tag.

prefix.summary: A widely-used SD1.5 photoreal merge.
A large SDXL merge aiming to be a clean, versatile all-round base.
Cagliostro Lab's anime-specialised SDXL fine-tune, trained with Danbooru-style tag prompting.
prefix.good_for: High-quality anime on SDXL (booru tag prompts)

prefix.summary: A popular anime/illustration line. The original is SD1.5; CounterfeitXL is SDXL.
prefix.notes: Base depends on the build (SD1.5 vs XL).

prefix.summary: A classic SD1.5 anime merge family (V3-V5). Soft, clean anime aesthetic.
An SDXL-family autoencoder. Prefer an fp16-fixed build if fp16 inference produces black/NaN output.
prefix.good_for: SDXL checkpoints without a baked-in VAE

prefix.summary: The Flux.1 / SD3-family autoencoder. Decodes latents from Flux and SD3 diffusion models.
An anime-tuned SD1.5 autoencoder (vivid colour, crisp lines). kl-f8-anime and OrangeMix VAE are the common ones.
A Stability SD1.5 autoencoder (the 840k-step MSE build is the community default; the EMA build is slightly different in tone).
prefix.good_for: SD1.5 checkpoints without a baked-in VAE

prefix.summary: A tiny distilled autoencoder (taesd = SD1.5, taesdxl = SDXL, taef1 = Flux). Decodes near-instantly at slightly reduced fidelity.
prefix.good_for: Fast live previews; not final-quality decode

prefix.summary: A community ESRGAN model favoured for sharp, detailed 4x photo upscales without heavy artefacting.
A popular ESRGAN upscaler that preserves texture and fine detail; a frequent default for realism.
prefix.good_for: Detailed 4x upscaling of photos and renders

prefix.summary: NMKD's ESRGAN upscalers (Siax, Superscale, etc.) — strong general-purpose 4x models.
Xintao Wang et al's Real-ESRGAN, trained for real-world degraded images. The _anime build targets line-art/illustration.
prefix.good_for: Robust real-world upscaling; _anime build for illustration

prefix.summary: An anime/illustration-tuned ESRGAN upscaler (clean lines, flat colour).
A super-resolution network (DAT / HAT / SwinIR / BSRGAN / SCUNet / LSDIR / SPAN / ATD families). The transformer-based ones (DAT, HAT, SwinIR) can beat ESRGAN on detail at higher cost.
An ESRGAN-architecture upscaler. The leading 2x/4x in the name is the scale factor.
Conditions generation on Canny edge maps. Strong, hard edge guidance.
prefix.good_for: Locking composition/outline to a reference's edges

prefix.summary: Conditions on a depth map (MiDaS / Depth-Anything). Preserves 3D layout and pose volume.
Conditions on a body/hand/face keypoint skeleton. Controls pose without constraining appearance.
Conditions on extracted line art. Good for inking/colouring and style-preserving redraws.
Conditions on rough scribbles/sketches. The loosest, most forgiving spatial guide.
Conditions on soft (HED/PiDiNet) edges — gentler than Canny, keeping more texture cues.
prefix.good_for: Edge guidance that retains soft detail

prefix.summary: Adds/repairs detail tile-by-tile, ignoring global prompt mismatch. The backbone of many high-res upscales.
Conditions inpainting on the surrounding image for seamless fills.
Conditions on a surface-normal map (NormalBAE). Controls fine surface orientation.
prefix.good_for: Preserving surface relief and lighting direction

prefix.summary: An all-in-one ControlNet handling many control types (canny/depth/pose/…) in a single model.
prefix.good_for: One model for mixed control types

prefix.summary: A specialised ControlNet (QR-code, MLSD lines, segmentation, instruct-pix2pix, shuffle, recolour, …). The name states the control type.
prefix.good_for: The specific control modality named in the file

prefix.summary: Google's T5-XXL encoder, used as the prompt encoder for Flux and SD3/3.5. fp8 builds halve VRAM at a small quality cost.
prefix.good_for: Flux / SD3 prompt encoding (pair with clip_l)

prefix.summary: OpenAI CLIP ViT-L/14 text encoder — the SD1.5 text tower, and the secondary encoder for SDXL / Flux / SD3.
prefix.good_for: SD1.5; SDXL/Flux/SD3 as the CLIP half (with t5xxl or clip_g)

prefix.summary: OpenCLIP ViT-bigG/14 text encoder — the larger of SDXL's two text towers (and used by SD3).
prefix.good_for: SDXL / SD3 prompt encoding (pair with clip_l)

prefix.summary: The multilingual UMT5-XXL encoder used by the Wan video models.
An LLM-based prompt encoder (e.g. LLaVA/LLaMA for HunyuanVideo). Pair with the matching CLIP encoder.
A Gemma-based prompt encoder (used by Lumina-Image 2.0 and similar).
A CLIP image encoder (ViT-H/14 or ViT-bigG/14). Turns a reference image into an embedding for IP-Adapter, unCLIP and style models.
A CLIP ViT-L/14 image encoder. Used by some IP-Adapter and unCLIP setups.
Black Forest Labs' 12B rectified-flow transformer family. Excellent prompt adherence and text rendering; needs a t5xxl + clip_l encoder and the ae VAE.
Stability's MMDiT model line (Large / Large-Turbo / Medium). Improved prompt following and typography over SDXL.
Stability's first MMDiT release (SD3 Medium). Uses a triple text encoder (clip_l + clip_g + t5xxl).
prefix.good_for: Prompt-following and text rendering on SD3

prefix.summary: Pony Diffusion V6 XL — an SDXL derivative with its own score_/rating prompt vocabulary. Huge ecosystem of Pony-specific LoRAs (NOT cross-compatible with vanilla SDXL).
prefix.good_for: Characters, anime/furry; use score_9-style quality tags

prefix.summary: An SDXL anime base trained on booru tags; a popular successor to Pony for illustration. LoRAs are usually trained per-base.
An SDXL anime base (Illustrious-derived) trained on Danbooru/e621 tags. Common base for recent anime LoRAs.
Stability's 2.6B two-stage model. The mainstream open base — large LoRA/ControlNet ecosystem. Turbo/Lightning variants run in 1-8 steps.
Stability's SD2.0/2.1 (OpenCLIP text encoder, 768px native). Largely superseded by SDXL.
The original 0.86B workhorse (512px native). Still the largest LoRA/embedding ecosystem and the lightest to run.
prefix.good_for: Low-VRAM generation; the deepest add-on ecosystem

prefix.summary: A DiT (diffusion transformer) family with strong prompt following, trained efficiently. PixArt-Σ targets up to 4K.
prefix.good_for: Prompt-faithful generation; an efficient DiT alternative

prefix.summary: A large open rectified-flow transformer (fal). Fully open, strong prompt adherence.
Kuaishou's bilingual (CN/EN) SDXL-architecture model with a GLM text encoder. Strong on Chinese prompts and text.
Tencent's Hunyuan line — HunyuanDiT (bilingual image DiT) and HunyuanVideo (large open video model).
Alibaba's Wan2.x video diffusion family (T2V / I2V). Pairs with the umt5 text encoder and a Wan VAE.
Lightricks' fast DiT video model — real-time-ish generation at modest resolution.
Genmo's open AsymmDiT video model, known for strong motion fidelity.
THUDM's open video DiT family (2B/5B), available in T2V and I2V variants.
NVIDIA's Cosmos world-foundation models for video/world simulation.
The Lumina (Next-DiT) family, using a Gemma text encoder. Strong prompt following.
prefix.good_for: Modern DiT generation with LLM-grade prompting

prefix.summary: Alibaba's Qwen-Image model — notable for accurate text rendering (including Chinese) and editing.
prefix.good_for: Images with lots of legible text; bilingual prompts

prefix.summary: HiDream's large open MoE-DiT image model with strong prompt adherence.
A Flux-Schnell-derived, fully open (Apache-2.0) model retrained for unrestricted fine-tuning.
Stability's Würstchen-v3 cascade — a highly-compressed latent space across stages A/B/C, cheap to fine-tune.
Stability's image-to-video model. Animates a still image into a short clip.
